# fieldClim

`fieldClim` is an R package for calculations of weather-station based
microclimate and micrometeorological parameters.

It provides functions for:

- estimating radiation properties,
- calculating latent and sensible heat fluxes,
- calculating turbulent heat flux diagnostics,
- calculating soil heat fluxes,
- estimating thermal and mechanical boundary-layer properties,
- organizing station data in a structured `weather_station` object.

The package was originally cloned from:

``` r
https://gitlab.uni-marburg.de/fb19/ag-bendix/fieldClim.git
```

This repository is a private development and migration repository. It is
currently under construction and should not be treated as a stable
public release.

## Installation

Install the current development version with:

``` r

pak::pak("https://github.com/gisma/migration-fieldclim.git")
```

Then load the package with:

``` r

library(fieldClim)
```

For private repository access, R must be authenticated against GitHub,
for example via a valid `GITHUB_PAT`.

## Current status

The package is under active consolidation. Some historical rendered HTML
vignettes included in the repository are archived outputs and should not
be treated as current numerical references.

In the Caldern metadata, `EC` means electric conductivity. The package
does not implement a complete Eddy Covariance processing chain.

This clone adds a stable workflow for microclimate energy-balance
calculations.

A small packaged teaching dataset is provided:

``` text
inst/extdata/caldern_wiese_2017-06-30.csv
```

It contains one complete 5-minute day with 288 observations. The full
Caldern raw dataset is intentionally not included in the package.

The consolidated sign convention is:

- `Rn > 0`: radiative energy input at the surface
- `G > 0`: heat flux into the soil
- `H > 0`: sensible heat flux away from the surface
- `LE > 0`: latent heat flux away from the surface

For the Priestley-Taylor teaching path:

``` text
H + LE = Rn - G
```

The Priestley-Taylor formulas were not changed. Documentation and tests
were updated to match the already implemented `Rn - G` convention.

A beginner-safe workflow is available through:

``` r

turb_flux_calc(weather_station, pt_only = TRUE)
```

This computes only:

``` text
sensible_priestley_taylor
latent_priestley_taylor
```

and avoids optional Penman, Bowen and Monin-Obukhov paths in first
teaching exercises.

## Consolidation changes in this branch

This branch includes small robustness fixes and documentation
clarifications:

`turb_flux_calc(weather_station, pt_only = TRUE)` provides a
beginner-safe teaching path. It computes only:

- `sensible_priestley_taylor`
- `latent_priestley_taylor`

This avoids the optional Penman, Bowen and Monin-Obukhov paths in first
teaching exercises.

[`turb_flux_calc()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_calc.md)
keeps the full method workflow available, including:

- Priestley-Taylor
- Bowen
- Monin-Obukhov
- Penman

Penman failures are now non-fatal inside
[`turb_flux_calc()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_calc.md).

[`latent_penman.weather_station()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/latent_penman.md)
was consolidated so that common `fieldClim` surface types, such as
`field`, can be mapped to Penman-compatible resistance classes.

Relative humidity handling in the Penman weather-station method was made
consistent:

- profile humidity is used when available,
- otherwise the standard `rh` field is used.

[`as.data.frame.weather_station()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/as.data.frame.weather_station.md)
now supports the current flat `weather_station` object structure.

Numeric warning checks in heat-flux functions were repaired so warnings
are based on calculated numeric values rather than broken logical
checks.

[`soil_attenuation()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/soil_attenuation.md)
argument forwarding was corrected.

Bowen denominator handling was made more robust.

The Caldern teaching vignette now uses the packaged one-day dataset. It:

- reads `"NULL"` as `NA`,
- parses timestamps explicitly with `Europe/Berlin`,
- avoids depending on the full raw CSV.

New teaching and documentation material was added for:

- energy-balance workflows,
- radiation checks,
- soil heat flux checks,
- additional package use cases.

Tests were added or updated for:

- the consolidated teaching dataset,
- `weather_station` handling,
- warning logic,
- soil attenuation,
- Bowen denominator behavior,
- Penman availability.

## What this package does not currently provide

`fieldClim` currently does not provide a full Eddy Covariance workflow.
It does not implement a complete chain including Reynolds decomposition,
covariance calculation, coordinate rotation, WPL correction, quality
control and 30-minute flux aggregation.

The current teaching workflow is therefore an energy-balance and
heat-flux-methods workflow, not an Eddy Covariance workflow.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`as.data.frame(`*`<weather_station>`*`)`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/as.data.frame.weather_station.md)
  : Create a data.frame from a weather station object
- [`bound_mech_avg()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/bound_mech_avg.md)
  : Mechanical internal boundary layer; average height.
- [`bound_mech_low()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/bound_mech_low.md)
  : Mechanical internal boundary layer; lowest height.
- [`bound_thermal_avg()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/bound_thermal_avg.md)
  : Thermal internal boundary layer.
- [`build_weather_station()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/build_weather_station.md)
  : Build a weather station object
- [`hum_absolute()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/hum_absolute.md)
  : Absolute humidity
- [`hum_evap_heat()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/hum_evap_heat.md)
  : Enthalpy of vaporization
- [`hum_moisture_gradient()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/hum_moisture_gradient.md)
  : Moisture gradient
- [`hum_precipitable_water()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/hum_precipitable_water.md)
  : Precipitable water
- [`hum_specific()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/hum_specific.md)
  : Specific humidity
- [`latent_bowen()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/latent_bowen.md)
  : Latent Heat using Bowen Method
- [`latent_monin()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/latent_monin.md)
  : Latent Heat using Monin-Obukhov length
- [`latent_penman()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/latent_penman.md)
  : Latent Heat Penman-Monteith Method
- [`latent_priestley_taylor()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/latent_priestley_taylor.md)
  : Latent Heat Priestley-Taylor Method
- [`plot_weather_station()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/plot_weather_station.md)
  : Plot Weather Station Data
- [`pos_anemometer_height()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pos_anemometer_height.md)
  : Necessary Anemometer Height
- [`pos_max_dist()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pos_max_dist.md)
  : Maximum Distance between Climate Station and Obstacle
- [`pos_min_dist()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pos_min_dist.md)
  : Minimum Distance between Climate Station and Obstacle
- [`pres_air_density()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pres_air_density.md)
  : Air Density
- [`pres_p()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pres_p.md)
  : Air Pressure
- [`pres_sat_vapor_p()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pres_sat_vapor_p.md)
  : Saturated Vapor Pressure
- [`pres_vapor_p()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/pres_vapor_p.md)
  : Vapor Pressure
- [`rad_bal()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_bal.md)
  : Total Radiation Balance
- [`rad_diffuse_in()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_diffuse_in.md)
  : Incoming Diffused Radiation
- [`rad_diffuse_out()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_diffuse_out.md)
  : Diffused Outgoing Radiation
- [`rad_emissivity_air()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_emissivity_air.md)
  : Emissivity of the Atmosphere
- [`rad_lw_bal()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_lw_bal.md)
  : Longwave Radiation Balance
- [`rad_lw_in()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_lw_in.md)
  : Longwave Incoming Radiation
- [`rad_lw_out()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_lw_out.md)
  : Longwave Outgoing Radiation
- [`rad_sw_bal()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_sw_bal.md)
  : Shortwave Radiation Balance
- [`rad_sw_in()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_sw_in.md)
  : Shortwave Incoming Radiation
- [`rad_sw_out()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_sw_out.md)
  : Shortwave Outgoing Radiation
- [`rad_sw_toa()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/rad_sw_toa.md)
  : Shortwave Radiation at Top of Atmosphere
- [`sensible_bowen()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sensible_bowen.md)
  : Sensible Heat using Bowen Method
- [`sensible_monin()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sensible_monin.md)
  : Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length
- [`sensible_priestley_taylor()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sensible_priestley_taylor.md)
  : Sensible Heat Priestley-Taylor Method
- [`soil_attenuation()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/soil_attenuation.md)
  : Soil Attenuation Length
- [`soil_heat_cap()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/soil_heat_cap.md)
  : Soil Volumetric Heat Capacity
- [`soil_heat_flux()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/soil_heat_flux.md)
  : Soil Heat Flux
- [`soil_thermal_cond()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/soil_thermal_cond.md)
  : Soil Thermal Conductivity
- [`sol_azimuth()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_azimuth.md)
  : Solar azimuth
- [`sol_day_angle()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_day_angle.md)
  : Day angle
- [`sol_declination()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_declination.md)
  : Solar declination
- [`sol_eccentricity()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_eccentricity.md)
  : Eccentricity factor
- [`sol_ecliptic_length()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_ecliptic_length.md)
  : Solar ecliptic length
- [`sol_elevation()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_elevation.md)
  : Solar elevation angle
- [`sol_hour_angle()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_hour_angle.md)
  : Solar hour angle
- [`sol_julian_day()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_julian_day.md)
  : Julian day
- [`sol_medium_anomaly()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_medium_anomaly.md)
  : Solar medium anomaly
- [`sol_medium_suntime()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_medium_suntime.md)
  : Solar medium suntime
- [`sol_time_formula()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/sol_time_formula.md)
  : Solar time formula
- [`temp_pot_temp()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/temp_pot_temp.md)
  : Potential Temperature
- [`terr_sky_view()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/terr_sky_view.md)
  : Sky View Factor
- [`terr_terrain_angle()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/terr_terrain_angle.md)
  : Terrain Angle
- [`trans_aerosol()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_aerosol.md)
  : Transmittance due to aerosols
- [`trans_air_mass_abs()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_air_mass_abs.md)
  : Absolute optical air mass
- [`trans_air_mass_rel()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_air_mass_rel.md)
  : Relative optical air mass
- [`trans_gas()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_gas.md)
  : Transmittance due to gas
- [`trans_ozone()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_ozone.md)
  : Transmittance due to ozone
- [`trans_rayleigh()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_rayleigh.md)
  : Transmittance due to rayleigh scattering
- [`trans_vapor()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/trans_vapor.md)
  : Transmittance due to water vapor
- [`turb_displacement()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_displacement.md)
  : Displacement height
- [`turb_flux_calc()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_calc.md)
  : Sensible and latent heat fluxes
- [`turb_flux_ex_quotient_imp()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_ex_quotient_imp.md)
  : Exchange quotient for impulse transmission
- [`turb_flux_ex_quotient_temp()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_ex_quotient_temp.md)
  : Exchange quotient for heat transmission
- [`turb_flux_grad_rich_no()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_grad_rich_no.md)
  : Gradient-Richardson-Number
- [`turb_flux_imp_exchange()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_imp_exchange.md)
  : Turbulent impulse exchange
- [`turb_flux_monin()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_monin.md)
  : Monin-Obhukov-Length
- [`turb_flux_stability()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_flux_stability.md)
  : Stability
- [`turb_roughness_length()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_roughness_length.md)
  : Roughness length
- [`turb_ustar()`](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/reference/turb_ustar.md)
  : Friction velocity

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [fieldClim: Use-Case-Workflows für Energiebilanz und
  Wärmeflussmethoden](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/articles/01_fieldclim-usecase-workflows.md):
- [fieldClim: weitere
  Use-Case-Workflows](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/articles/02-fieldclim-additional-usecases-fixed2.md):
- [FieldClim Package Update
  Documentation](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/articles/03-fieldclim-package-update-documentation.md):
- [fieldClim: Check radiation and soil heat
  flux](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/articles/04-Check-rad-and-soil.md):
- [Introduction to
  fieldClim](https://gisma.github.io/migration-fieldclim/articles/fieldClim.md):
